headline: Ostrich Towne setback: Avestruz closed in November, Italian concept pending — district enters summer on two confirmed anchors
paragraphs: Avestruz Tapas & Tequila — one of three restaurants previously cited as open at Ostrich Towne — closed unexpectedly in November 2025 after its management team resigned with minimal notice to investors, leaving employees without their final paychecks. Developer Joe Nachtrab stepped in to cover those wages. A sign on the door announced an Italian dining concept from Chef Paul Matthews — who also operates Riverside Barbeque Company — as the planned successor. That concept was targeted for early 2026 but had not confirmed an opening date as of late May.

With Avestruz gone, Ostrich Towne currently has two confirmed food-and-beverage anchors: Molly's Irish Pub and Riverside Barbeque Company. CuriosiTEA Emporium and the Italian concept remain the next expected openings, but neither has a confirmed date. The district enters the Watershed Weekend foot-traffic window (June 11–14) at a narrower tenant base than the spring framing suggested — and with a fourth tenant now overdue.
why_it_matters: The Ostrich Towne thesis requires enough tenant variety to sustain dwell-time and return visits. Two anchors can hold weekend bar traffic, but it is a thinner summer case than the three-tenant baseline that had been tracked — and well short of the four-to-five-tenant mix needed to lock in daytime visits. The Italian concept, whenever it opens, is the pivotal signal: if Chef Paul Matthews delivers a second concept that works, Nachtrab has a development story that can absorb setbacks and rebuild. Watch for a confirmed opening date and for CuriosiTEA's status before Watershed Weekend.

sources.title: WTOL — Avestruz closes unexpectedly, employees face pay delay
